text:
If you were hoping the smoke would stick around and ruin your Friday, tough luck, it's cleared out and today is genuinely gorgeous: sunny, light northwest wind, a high right around 81. Air quality's back to good, so no excuses to stay inside. The lake's calm too, waves 1 to 2 feet, water sitting at 72, about as inviting as it gets. Enjoy it, because the pattern flips fast.

Saturday is the one to plan around. The forecasters have actually upped the severe threat overnight, and both the timing and the smoke are messy, showers arriving as early as 9am, then a real chance of thunderstorms rolling in through the afternoon and evening, with wind on the lake building into the 15 to 25 knot range by night. The models are split on just how hot it gets first (anywhere from the mid 70s to mid 80s), but everyone agrees the second half of the day gets wet and gusty, with three quarters of an inch possible overnight. Get your outdoor plans done in the morning, and I'd have the boat back at the dock well before early afternoon.
